rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. GREGORY YOCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Dismissing Entire Action With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 4, 2011, line 3. PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Dismissing Entire Action With Prejudice. Motion for reconsideration denied. Plaintiff has failed to follow proper procedure in requesting this relief. Further, the court had jurisdiction to dismiss the action with prejudice and plaintiff fails to provide an affadavit citing new facts or legal authority to justify granting of this motion. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW ORLANDO VS. SUPPERCLUB SAN FRANCISCO, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel The Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 4, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ORLANDO's Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents is granted. Plaintiff has made the required showing for production of the Wu personnel file under Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 C3d. 844, 859-862. Despite the fact that the reqested witness statements taken by defendants' employees were later transmitted to defendants' attorneys, they do not fall under the attorney-client privilege Nacht & Lewis Architects Inc. v. Superior Court, (1996) 47 CA 4th 214, 218. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARCHETTI CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. MARSHFIELD DOORSYSTEMS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 4, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT MARSHFIELD DOORSYSTEMS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The contractual limitations period of 1 year is reasonable. Accordingly, the 1st cause of action is time barred. The cause of action for negligence and negligent interference are barred under the "economic loss rule" which bars recovery for any damages in tort unless those damages are accompanied by physical injury to plaintiff or plaintiff's property. Plaintiff's request for leave to amend to add facts to conform to proof is denied. Plaintiff fails to explain why his did not file this request prior to the motion for summary judgment and fails to provide a declaration about any newly discovered evidence. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 4, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. It has long been held in California law that an essential element to a cause of action for medical malpractice is a physician-patient relationship giving rise to a duty of care. Mero v. Sadoff, (1995) 31 CA 4th 1466, 1471. Plaintiff and defendant never had a physician-patient relationship and thus plaintiff may mot bring this cause of action against this defendant. The Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The demurrer to the 6th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The demurrer to the 7th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 4, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANT JONATHAN LEICHTLING, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to Strike is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to the two paragraphs that relate to wrongful death because ¿California cases have uniformly held that damages for mental and emotional distress, including grief and sorrow, are not recoverable in a wrongful death action.¿ Krouse v. Graham, (1977) 19 Cal.3rd 59, 72. As to the medical malpractice paragraphs, the motion is denied. Plaintiff may be able to recover for the negligence cause of actions. HOWEVER, as stated above, plaintiff cannot recover from this defendant because plaintiff and this defendant did not have a physician-patient relationship. The Motion is granted as to these paragraphs. Because the Estate does not appear to have a capacity to bring this cause of action, it follows that it would be proper to strike its prayer for damages. The Motion is granted as to the prayers for relief in the Medical Malpractice and Emotional Distress causes of action because they are duplicative and properly asserted in the Wrongful Death cause of action. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 4, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ANNA SZARY M.D., RUEDI THOENI, M.D., ESTHER CHEN, M.D., MARGARET SALMON, M.D., BORY KEA, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Standing Issue: Sustained with Leave to amend. The estate does not appear to have any capacity to sue. In re Peterson, 156 Cal.App.4th 676, 679 n. 1. The Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. This cause of action impermissibly alleges two causes of action ¿ wrongful death and negligent hiring. The Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action in sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead further facts of extreme conduct to support a gross negligence claim. The Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The cause of action for breach of express warranty seems to only apply to products, not services. This cause of action is unsupportable based on the facts of this case. See CACI 1230 (holding that an integral element of this Cause of Action is that the ¿product¿ did not function correctly or did not meet the quality of the description/sample/model.) The Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead more sufficient facts to support this claim as to specific defendants. The Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. This is not an independent cause of action but a form of damages that may be recovered under the negligence cause of actions. The Demurrer to 7th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Loss of companionship is not a recognizable cause of action but a form of non-economic damages. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 4, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANT LAUREN FRIEDLY, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer to the 6th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The Demurrer to the 7th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KAMALA D. HARRIS, ATTORNEY GENERAL OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Case Re Compliance With Investigative Subpoenas. (Per 03-03-11 Order)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 4, 2011, Line 9. Order To Show Case Re Compliance With Investigative Subpoenas. Petition to Compel Compliance is granted. Petitioner has shown that the subpoenas were regularly issued in accordance with the Government Code. Further Respondent should provide a privilege log with regard to withheld documents. The Petitioner has also shown that the documents are reasonably relevant to its investigation. Respondents objections under HIPPA are unfounded. 45CFR 164.512(e) & (f) permit disclosures in response to law enforcement and administrative subpoenas. Respondent should provide complete, unredacted files and information related to medical loss and premiums. = (302/LMG)
